# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a multi-service application container that runs a web server (port 80), API server (port 8080), database (port 5432), and Redis (port 6379). Expose all necessary ports so the services can communicate. This is for our all-in-one development container.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:latest

# Install necessary packages
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y nginx curl gnupg2 lsb-release postgresql redis-server

# Configure Nginx to run on port 80
RUN echo "daemon off;" >> /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
COPY default.conf /etc/nginx/conf.d/

# Install Node.js (for API server)
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_14.x | bash - && \
    apt-get install -y nodejs

# Set up API server
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm install
COPY . .

# Configure PostgreSQL
RUN service postgresql start && \
    sudo -u postgres psql -c "ALTER USER postgres PASSWORD 'password';" && \
    service postgresql stop

EXPOSE 80 8080 5432 6379

# Start all services
CMD service postgresql start && \
    service redis-server start && \
    service nginx start && \
    npm start

# Copy web server config
COPY webserver.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default

# Environment variables for PostgreSQL
ENV POSTGRES_DB=db POSTGRES_USER=postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD=password
